div.head: The Ambassador in Great Britain (Houghton) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 267. Referring to my number 253, November 17, 1 p.m.Not printed. Yesterday afternoon SterlingFrederick A. Sterling, counselor of the American Embassy. was called to the Foreign Office by Tyrrell.Sir William G. Tyrrell, British Permanent Under Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs. The situation in China was again reviewed by Tyrrell, and he reviewed the suggestion that the additional surtax of 2½ percent should be granted by the powers with no reservations and with no conditions regarding likin or debt consolidation.
His insistence that it was necessary to make this concession immediately constituted the importance of the interview.
